name: Fukui Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the Sea of Japan and the prefecture's coastal heritage
purity and the snowy winters of the region

description: A navy-blue field charged at the centre with a white emblem stylising the katakana フキ, the abbreviated rendering of Fukui.
history: The prefectural symbol and flag were officially adopted on 1 March 1952, with the white emblem on a navy ground.

features.symbolism: a stylised abstraction of the katakana フ (fu) and キ (ki), the first two katakana of Fukui, arranged into a rounded form evoking flight and forward progress.
